w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Compliance to General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
The Process Definitions have been documented to comply with the General Data Protection Regulations.
create engaging visual concepts and supporting graphics to illustrate key sales messages, data, and differentiators in print and digital pursuit deliverables with a consistent and compelling style.
is to auto generate the Graphic Control number
Primary tool for Pursuit Services graphics support on Word response documents
submitting and tracking progress of graphic requests, and inventory management for future reference/reuse
. It is also used for storage
of completed graphic files, associated templates, and production materials
Review RFX/project scope, design & submission requirements
Consult with deal team to gather and assess client insights and win themes
Discuss and brainstorm with deal team for materials to support submission/meeting
Create document templates complying with client and RFX requirements/branding
Leverage repository of reusable graphics/assets to create custom graphics
Use the Graphics tracker to track status for word only
Create graphics tracker to track status of individual graphics files
Update graphics tracker where graphics modifications
Paste completed graphics files to Word file
Use iterative review cycle to refine graphics based on input from deal team
Format final documents and conduct quality check
Prepare files for final print production or digital submission
Confirm all final files have been uploaded for archiving/harvesting
as specified in the below table.
This section is to provide a list of Applications to be used in the Solution (To add Env specific from PAD)
Type of Application (Web, Windows, Citrix, Mainframe, etc.)
Activities the Applications is used in
Designers and other Graphic Tracker users need to have Member access granted in MS Teams.
This section to cover all relevant metric
ge the process performance for all activities in scope
The above table is indicative only and actual timelines may vary from deal to deal. Also, we expect the volume handled to increase by 1
Please mention details of any Data Protection/regulatory requirements that the process is required to comply with
Add Personal and Sensitive Personal Data (PD/SPD) specific to the Process
Automated Control Number prepopulated on adding new line items to the Graphic Tracker.
The Graphic tracker will be enabled on individual
Multiple designers can be supporting one deal and hence will be leveraging this tracker which is placed in the common team’s folder.
All Graphic must be modified in some way to be incorporated into the proposal response and must be logged for tracking purposes
should be generated even if multiple designers are creating it, this should be based on the last created number.
is created when the submitter or the graphic designer
reference and identify graphics created by Pursuit Services
All Graphic must be modified in some way to be incorporated into the proposal response and must be logged for tracking purposes.
be sent to the designer that a new item
has been added to the tracker.
he designer updates the tracker that the Graphic is
Designer notifies the submitter that this is now completed, reviewer will review and
update status of Graphic Tracker Request Item if any edits or changes needed
Graphic tracker is updated to complete status once received confirmation from requestor that this can be incorporated into the proposal response and no further edits are required.
Graphic Tracker is created for all word requests.
eeds to be available as a tab at the top of the Teams
All deal team members will have access to this Teams channel
available in the Graphic Teams Channel.
The theme to be aligned in Accenture colour. (Shared sample colour with the developer)
Landing page to have three options
Add/Update requests in existing Graphics Tracker
Teams channel link which would be a perquisite to
The control number naming using the following convention:
Opportunity ID number of the project from the
Year is a two-digit representation of the calendar year of the response submission
Order is the unique graphic number generated by the Graphics Tracker. This number will always be four numeric characters (e.g., 0001, 0999)
Remove the option to delete items from the tool.
Add an option to create a new item in the tool.
The "Submitted by" field should auto-fill with the submitter’s
The tool should have the ability to
Currently, the tool refreshes manually. The system should refresh it automatically.
to guide users on folder locations and standard naming conventions. The
Restrict access to users who are not working on the deal. Only assigned users should have access. (The dropdown should appear only when access is enabled.)
will be accessible to everyone in Accenture, but the
will be restricted. Users will only gain access to deal details once they are assigned to the deal.
to change the Unique ID that is autogenerated.
availability of Opportunity ID details will impact the Control Number creation
Graphic Designer should be able to turn this notification option on or off)
When a Graphic is submitted a notification must be sent to the Graphic Designer
Subject: New Graphics for Proposal - [Opportunity Name]
A new Graphic request has been submitted.
If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to the Submitter.
Updates needed request has been submitted for the below
If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to the Submitter.
When Progress changes to “Completed” send email to
We are pleased to inform you that the graphics for your proposal have been completed. Below are the details of the completed graphics:
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to reach out to the graphic design team.
If you require any modifications to the completed graphics, you can submit an Updates Needed request. Please provide the necessary details and instructions for the modifications.
Thank you for your submission and collaboration.
Annexure A — Clarifications from BA Review

The following questions were raised by the Business Analyst (BA) Agent during the PDD review. Each question has been answered by the Business Transformation (BT) team and the answers form part of the approved scope for downstream Architecture, TDD and Development work.
Q1. What is the exact format and structure of the autonumber (e.g., delimiter, padding, character length for Opp ID/Year/Order)? Should the 'Order' be sequential per opportunity, per y…

What is the exact format and structure of the autonumber (e.g., delimiter, padding, character length for Opp ID/Year/Order)? Should the 'Order' be sequential per opportunity, per year, or globally, and how should collisions or re-runs be prevented when graphics are modified (where the PDD states 'a new slide within the file is created for each modification')?
Order is the unique graphic number generated by the Graphics Tracker. This number will always be four numeric characters (e.g., 0001, 0999)
000007 is opportunity ID, 26 is the year like 2026, 0001 is the order
Q2. How should the autonumber behave for modified/revised graphics versus net-new graphics? Does a modification inherit the parent autonumber with a version suffix, or is a brand new c…

How should the autonumber behave for modified/revised graphics versus net-new graphics? Does a modification inherit the parent autonumber with a version suffix, or is a brand new control number issued? The success criteria mention iterative review cycles but do not clarify versioning rules.
Its not editable once generated it's auto populate everywhere in read only mode
Q3. What is the integration approach with the existing Graphics Tracker tool? Is this a plugin/macro within the tracker, an external service, or a replacement module? What APIs, data s…

What is the integration approach with the existing Graphics Tracker tool? Is this a plugin/macro within the tracker, an external service, or a replacement module? What APIs, data schemas, or file formats (Excel, SharePoint list, database) does the current tracker expose for read/write of the control number field?
The Graphic Tracker Autonumbering Power App is built on Microsoft Power Platform and integrates with Microsoft Teams, Dataverse and SharePoint
Q4. Given the volume (~58,050 graphics/month) and a 10-20% expected growth in FY25, what are the throughput, concurrency, and latency requirements for the autonumbering service? Are th…

Given the volume (~58,050 graphics/month) and a 10-20% expected growth in FY25, what are the throughput, concurrency, and latency requirements for the autonumbering service? Are there peak-hour spikes (e.g., end-of-quarter pursuit deadlines) that require burst capacity beyond the stated 2-minute average handling time?

No peak hours spikes will be there because only 300 max users will be using the app and even if there is more users using application will not impact performance as we have simple action items by generating numbers we are sending emails for it.we dont have complicated logic or api's we are calling here
Q5. What validation rules and exception handling are required when the Opportunity ID is missing, malformed, duplicated across markets, or when a graphic request spans multiple opportu…

What validation rules and exception handling are required when the Opportunity ID is missing, malformed, duplicated across markets, or when a graphic request spans multiple opportunities? Who is the business owner for resolving these exceptions, and what is the fallback if the autonumber service is unavailable?

This numbers are autogenerated so failure scenario will not come this can be happen only if power automate plateform will be down and below 1 business rule we have : - Team and Channel dropdowns display only Teams/Channels user is part of
Q6. What audit trail, logging, and reporting capabilities are required for compliance and harvesting/archiving (referenced in the success criteria)? Specifically, who can view, modify,…

What audit trail, logging, and reporting capabilities are required for compliance and harvesting/archiving (referenced in the success criteria)? Specifically, who can view, modify, or override an assigned autonumber, and what RBAC model applies across the 262 in-scope FTEs across all markets?
No its not required as it is very simple and straightforward application
Q7. The success criteria mention notifications to designer/submitter/reviewer at multiple status transitions, but the scope only covers autonumbering. Is notification orchestration in-…

The success criteria mention notifications to designer/submitter/reviewer at multiple status transitions, but the scope only covers autonumbering. Is notification orchestration in-scope for this automation, or is it handled by the existing Graphics Tracker? If in-scope, what channels (email, Teams, in-app) and SLAs apply?

After successful run below email will trigger and its the indicate of successful process:Email Notifications – In edit list, user can have option to toggle between email notifications as on/off to receive email notifications whenever there is a status change with that list item to the author. The field will be a toggle and will be called “Email status updates to Author”.
We are pleased to inform you that the graphic for your proposal has been completed. Below are the details of the completed graphic:
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to reach out to the assigned Graphic Designer.

If you require any modifications to the completed graphics, you can edit the status of the graphic to “Status.” Please provide the necessary details and instructions for the modifications through this link - Go to the tracker
Note: This is an automatically generated email, please do not reply.
All BA clarifications addressed. PDD approved for Architecture phase.

This document is a system-generated, sign-off-ready version of the Process Definition Document. It preserves the original PDD body (including diagrams and screenshots) and appends the authoritative clarifications captured during the BA review cycle.
Annexure A — Clarifications from BA Review

The following questions were raised by the Business Analyst (BA) Agent during the PDD review. Each question has been answered by the Business Transformation (BT) team and the answers form part of the approved scope for downstream Architecture, TDD and Development work.
Q1. What is the exact format and structure of the autonumber (e.g., delimiter, padding, character length for Opp ID/Year/Order)? Should the 'Order' be sequential per opportunity, per y…

What is the exact format and structure of the autonumber (e.g., delimiter, padding, character length for Opp ID/Year/Order)? Should the 'Order' be sequential per opportunity, per year, or globally, and how should collisions or re-runs be prevented when graphics are modified (where the PDD states 'a new slide within the file is created for each modification')?
Order is the unique graphic number generated by the Graphics Tracker. This number will always be four numeric characters (e.g., 0001, 0999)
000007 is opportunity ID, 26 is the year like 2026, 0001 is the order
Q2. How should the autonumber behave for modified/revised graphics versus net-new graphics? Does a modification inherit the parent autonumber with a version suffix, or is a brand new c…

How should the autonumber behave for modified/revised graphics versus net-new graphics? Does a modification inherit the parent autonumber with a version suffix, or is a brand new control number issued? The success criteria mention iterative review cycles but do not clarify versioning rules.
Its not editable once generated it's auto populate everywhere in read only mode
Q3. What is the integration approach with the existing Graphics Tracker tool? Is this a plugin/macro within the tracker, an external service, or a replacement module? What APIs, data s…

What is the integration approach with the existing Graphics Tracker tool? Is this a plugin/macro within the tracker, an external service, or a replacement module? What APIs, data schemas, or file formats (Excel, SharePoint list, database) does the current tracker expose for read/write of the control number field?
The Graphic Tracker Autonumbering Power App is built on Microsoft Power Platform and integrates with Microsoft Teams, Dataverse and SharePoint
Q4. Given the volume (~58,050 graphics/month) and a 10-20% expected growth in FY25, what are the throughput, concurrency, and latency requirements for the autonumbering service? Are th…

Given the volume (~58,050 graphics/month) and a 10-20% expected growth in FY25, what are the throughput, concurrency, and latency requirements for the autonumbering service? Are there peak-hour spikes (e.g., end-of-quarter pursuit deadlines) that require burst capacity beyond the stated 2-minute average handling time?

No peak hours spikes will be there because only 300 max users will be using the app and even if there is more users using application will not impact performance as we have simple action items by generating numbers we are sending emails for it.we dont have complicated logic or api's we are calling here
Q5. What validation rules and exception handling are required when the Opportunity ID is missing, malformed, duplicated across markets, or when a graphic request spans multiple opportu…

What validation rules and exception handling are required when the Opportunity ID is missing, malformed, duplicated across markets, or when a graphic request spans multiple opportunities? Who is the business owner for resolving these exceptions, and what is the fallback if the autonumber service is unavailable?

This numbers are autogenerated so failure scenario will not come this can be happen only if power automate plateform will be down and below 1 business rule we have : - Team and Channel dropdowns display only Teams/Channels user is part of
Q6. What audit trail, logging, and reporting capabilities are required for compliance and harvesting/archiving (referenced in the success criteria)? Specifically, who can view, modify,…

What audit trail, logging, and reporting capabilities are required for compliance and harvesting/archiving (referenced in the success criteria)? Specifically, who can view, modify, or override an assigned autonumber, and what RBAC model applies across the 262 in-scope FTEs across all markets?
No its not required as it is very simple and straightforward application
Q7. The success criteria mention notifications to designer/submitter/reviewer at multiple status transitions, but the scope only covers autonumbering. Is notification orchestration in-…

The success criteria mention notifications to designer/submitter/reviewer at multiple status transitions, but the scope only covers autonumbering. Is notification orchestration in-scope for this automation, or is it handled by the existing Graphics Tracker? If in-scope, what channels (email, Teams, in-app) and SLAs apply?

After successful run below email will trigger and its the indicate of successful process:Email Notifications – In edit list, user can have option to toggle between email notifications as on/off to receive email notifications whenever there is a status change with that list item to the author. The field will be a toggle and will be called “Email status updates to Author”.
We are pleased to inform you that the graphic for your proposal has been completed. Below are the details of the completed graphic:
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to reach out to the assigned Graphic Designer.

If you require any modifications to the completed graphics, you can edit the status of the graphic to “Status.” Please provide the necessary details and instructions for the modifications through this link - Go to the tracker
Note: This is an automatically generated email, please do not reply.

This document is a system-generated, sign-off-ready version of the Process Definition Document. It preserves the original PDD body (including diagrams and screenshots) and appends the authoritative clarifications captured during the BA review cycle.
